name: Ejulve - Gargallo - Las Calderas – circular route through the Cultural Park of Maestrazgo (PR-TE 93)
name_original: Ejulve - Gargallo - Las Calderas – circular por el Parque Cultural del Maestrazgo (PR-TE 93)
region_intro: Hiking around Ejulve offers exploration within a mountainous landscape at the headwaters of the Guadalopillo river, nestled at the foot of the Sierra de Majalinos. The region is characterized by steep slopes, rocky outcrops, and riparian forests, providing a diverse natural environment. Part of the Maestrazgo Cultural Park, the area features well-marked trails and numerous natural fountains. Hikers can experience varied terrain, from river valleys to panoramic viewpoints over the surrounding mountains. Best hiking trails around Ejulve The most popular hiking route is Valloré Viewpoint Trail, a 2.4 miles (3.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 15 minutes to complete. This easy route leads to spectacular views from wooden walkways along a picturesque gorge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ejulve – Ejulve Village loop from Ejulve, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This circular route explores the village surroundings and takes approximately 3 hours 35 minutes. Local hikers also love the Ejulve - Gargallo - Las Calderas – circular route through the Cultural Park of Maestrazgo (PR-TE 93), a 15.1 miles (24.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Ejulve is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and panoramic viewpoints, with options for different ability levels. The network of trails allows exploration of the region's natural and cultural heritage. The routes in Ejulve are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.97 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Ejulve's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ejulve?
A: Ejulve offers a diverse range of hiking opportunities, with over 20 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the mountainous landscape.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy walk?
A: Yes, Ejulve has several easy hiking options. There are 10 trails classified as easy, perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed experience. One popular easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974493780/vallore-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular views from wooden walkways along a picturesque gorge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Ejulve?
A: The terrain around Ejulve is characterized by its mountainous landscape, featuring steep slopes, rocky outcrops, and riparian forests along the Guadalopillo river. You'll encounter varied environments, from river valleys to panoramic viewpoints, reflecting its location at the foot of the Sierra de Majalinos and within the Maestrazgo Cultural Park.

Q: Are there any trails with impressive viewpoints in Ejulve?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974493780/vallore-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers fantastic views from its wooden walkways. Additionally, the area around Órganos de Montoro provides spectacular viewpoints like the Mirador de los Órganos, offering panoramic vistas over Las Hoces del Guadalope and the Boca del Infierno straits. The Mirador del Alto Maestrazgo also provides a vast mountain 'skyline'.

Q: Can I find trails that feature waterfalls in the Ejulve area?
A: Yes, the Ejulve area features natural water attractions. You can discover a seasonal waterfall in the Barranco de Cueva Muñoz. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Barranco de la Hoz Mala on the Guadalope River&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the vicinity.

Q: Are there any caves or unique geological features to explore while hiking?
A: Ejulve's surroundings are rich in subterranean landscapes, with nearly 40 cataloged caves and sinkholes. The Cueva del Recuenco is a notable spot for those interested in speleology. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grutas de Cristal&lt;/a&gt;, which are unique geological formations in the area.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Ejulve?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the presence of 10 easy trails suggests there are suitable options for families. These easier routes often feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them more accessible for children and those who prefer a gentler pace. The natural fountains and riparian forests can also be engaging for younger hikers.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Ejulve?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in natural areas like Ejulve, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas within the Maestrazgo Cultural Park to ensure compliance and respect for wildlife and other hikers.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Ejulve?
A: Yes, circular routes are a great way to experience the diverse landscapes of Ejulve. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31441384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ejulve – Ejulve Village loop from Ejulve&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.4-mile path that explores the village surroundings. Another challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981496889/ejulve-gargallo-las-calderas-circular-route-through-the-cultural-park-of-maestrazgo-pr-te-93" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ejulve - Gargallo - Las Calderas – circular route through the Cultural Park of Maestrazgo (PR-TE 93)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ejulve?
A: Ejulve is an excellent destination for outdoor activities due to its diverse natural landscape. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but the mountainous terrain and riparian forests provide cooler spots. Winter hiking is possible, but some higher elevation trails might be affected by snow or colder conditions.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Ejulve?
A: As a rural area popular for hiking, Ejulve typically offers parking options near trailheads and within the village itself. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, visitors usually find designated areas or roadside parking convenient to access the various routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ejulve?
A: The trails in Ejulve are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an impressive average rating of 4.97 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to panoramic viewpoints, and appreciate the well-marked paths that allow for exploration of the region's natural and cultural heritage.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover along the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, Ejulve's natural environment is rich with attractions. You can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Footbridges&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Pitarque River&lt;/a&gt;, and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de Crespol Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features numerous natural fountains and monumental trees, adding to the scenic beauty of your hikes.
tips: On the banks of the Guadalopillo River and at the foot of the Sierra de Majalinos rises this picturesque and quiet village typical of the interior of Teruel. In the heart of the Maestrazgo Cultural Park, this town offers many alternative hiking routes to tour this beautiful area of Aragon.
It is a recreational area with everything you need to spend a nice day next to this small but picturesque lake located very close to the town of Gargallo.

We are in the town of Gargallo, located at the foot of Muela Alta. Another of the beautiful towns in Teruel where we highlight the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Piedad, the Hermitage of San Blas or the Nacimiento del Río Escuriza.
